document.title: TkVillage System Manual
document.purpose: Explain how to use TkVillage, with LLM coding agents as the primary audience and human readers as the secondary audience.
author: Codex

audience.primary: LLM coding agents creating, modifying, testing, or debugging TkVillage applications.
audience.secondary: Human Python programmers who want a compact operational manual for the TkVillage kernel.
reader-assumptions: Reader has some familiarity with tkinter widgets and Toplevel windows.
Reader understands that TkVillage is a small runtime kernel, not a complete GUI framework.

orientation.one-sentence-summary: TkVillage is a lightweight procedural Tkinter host runtime built around a hidden root, summonable Toplevel windows, reducer-owned state dictionaries, queued semantic events, explicit effects, a periodic tick loop, and debug snapshots.
current-scope: singleton, per-key, and free-instance window policies
debug snapshots and a minimal debug dashboard

mental-model.runtime: The runtime is the shared host organism. It is stored in module-level data under tkvillage.app, commonly imported as rt.
mental-model.g: rt.g is the small runtime dictionary for scalar runtime values and root/project handles: name, root, project_dir, running flags, tick settings, and log limits.
mental-model.registries: Named module-level dictionaries hold registries for window kinds, live windows, instance-key lookups, targets, config declarations, config values, runtime objects, workers, debug state, and ids.
mental-model.hidden-root: TkVillage creates one tkinter.Tk root and withdraws it. Visible application windows are tkinter.Toplevel widgets.
mental-model.window-kind: A window kind is a registered template: it declares multiplicity, creation function, initial state function, reducer, projection function, and optional lifecycle hooks.
mental-model.window-record: A live window record connects a Toplevel, window_id, instance_key, reducer state, event queue, widgets, and lifecycle metadata.
mental-model.target: A target is anything that can receive queued runtime input. Windows are targets, and services may also be targets.
mental-model.event: An event is a JSON-friendly dictionary representing application meaning. Callbacks should usually post events rather than mutate state directly.
mental-model.reducer: A reducer receives rt, state, and event, then returns new_state and effects.
mental-model.effect: An effect is an explicit request for runtime work outside the reducer, such as posting another event, sending a message, summoning a window, closing a window, logging, or showing a message box.
mental-model.projection: Projection updates widgets from reducer state. Projection is downstream from state and should not secretly decide semantic truth.
mental-model.tick-loop: The tick loop drains service queues and window queues, runs reducers, routes effects, runs on_tick hooks, and projects dirty windows.

normal-workflow.description: Call create_app() once near program start. This resets the runtime, resolves the project directory, creates the hidden root by default, loads config, and registers debug windows.

Optionally declare configuration keys before loading or using values. If create_app() has already loaded config, call load_config() again after declarations when you need persisted values applied to new declarations.
Register each window kind with a stable name, multiplicity, create function, make_initial_state function, reducer, and projection.
Call summon_window() to create or raise the first visible Toplevel window.
Call run() to start the periodic tick loop and enter tkinter mainloop.

minimal-window-module-contract.summary: A TkVillage window module should be easy for an LLM to inspect. Keep the window kind name, event names, initial state, create function, reducer, projection, and register function visible.

recommended-symbols.window-kind: A stable string constant such as MAIN_WINDOW = "main".
recommended-symbols.event-types: Uppercase semantic names such as SAVE_REQUESTED or INCREMENT_CLICKED.
recommended-symbols.make_initial_state: Function returning a JSON-friendly state dictionary.
recommended-symbols.create: Function that builds widgets and stores important widget handles in record["widgets"].
recommended-symbols.reduce_event: Function that handles semantic events and returns new_state, effects.
recommended-symbols.project: Function that updates widgets from record["state"].

minimal-window-module-contract.callback-rule: Tkinter callbacks should usually post semantic events with post_event_to_window(record["window_id"], event).
minimal-window-module-contract.state-rule: Reducer state should not store Tk widgets, Toplevels, function references, open files, threads, queues, or other live Python runtime objects.
minimal-window-module-contract.widget-rule: Runtime-only widget handles belong in record["widgets"].

runtime-state.reset-rule: reset_app() preserves dictionary and list identities. Do not write code that depends on replacing these containers.
runtime-state.data-dictionary: See docs/raw/0015__data-dictionary_app.json for detailed runtime data definitions.

singleton.meaning: At most one live window exists for the window kind.
singleton.summon-behavior: summon_window() raises the existing window if present, otherwise realizes a new one.

per-key.meaning: At most one live window exists for each caller-provided key.
per-key.summon-behavior: summon_window(kind, key=...) reuses a matching live window or realizes a new one.

free-instance.meaning: Each summon creates a fresh independent window.
free-instance.summon-behavior: summon_window() always realizes a new live window.

embedded-and-modal.current-status: Names are recognized as multiplicity values, but v1 raises NotImplementedError for embedded and modal registrations.

sequence: Widget callback posts a semantic event to a target inbox.
For each window event, reduce_window_event() calls the window kind reducer.
Runtime stores new_state in the window record and target state.
project_dirty_windows() calls project() for dirty windows.

services-and-message-routing.service-target: A service target is a non-window target with an inbox, handler, state dictionary, and active flag.
services-and-message-routing.handler-contract: handler(rt, target, event) returns effects or None.
services-and-message-routing.use-case: Use services for app-level coordinators, autosave managers, debug tools, or other non-window participants that should receive queued input.

important-behavior: set_config() declares an undeclared key automatically using an inferred type.
path values are pathlib.Path objects in memory and strings in config.json.
choice values must be included in choices.
bool strings true/yes/on/1 become True; false/no/off/0 become False.

debugging.toggle: toggle_debug() flips rt.debug["enabled"]. When turning on, it summons the TkVillage runtime dashboard.
debugging.keyboard-shortcut: Ctrl+Shift+D is installed on the root by create_app() when a root exists.
debugging.clipboard: copy_debug_snapshot() writes the JSON snapshot text to the Tk clipboard when a root exists.

testing.test-mode: create_app(test_mode=True) causes tick_once() to re-raise tick exceptions instead of only logging them.
testing.manual-ticks: Use tick_once() or run_ticks(count) to advance the runtime without entering mainloop.
testing.run-until: testing.run_until(predicate, timeout_ms=1000, max_ticks=1000) advances ticks until a condition is true or a limit is reached.
testing.tk-availability: Tests that create Tk roots should be prepared to skip when the host Python/Tcl/Tk install is broken or unavailable.

llm-coding-guidelines: Keep window modules obvious and inspectable.
Define semantic event names before writing callbacks.
Post events from callbacks; do not bury important application behavior inside widget commands.
Reducers should copy or construct new semantic state and return effects.
Projection should update widgets from state and avoid becoming authority.
Do not put Tk widgets or Toplevels in reducer state.
Use rt only for runtime registries and debugging, not as a place to hide domain logic.
Prefer small dictionaries, explicit keys, and stable symbolic strings.
When adding a public function, update docs/raw/0022__tkvillage-system-api-reference.json.

common-pitfalls.pitfall: Registering a per-key window kind and summoning it without key.
Putting tkinter widget objects into reducer state.
common-pitfalls.result: State is no longer JSON-clean or debug-friendly.

common-pitfalls.pitfall: Calling project code directly inside a reducer.
common-pitfalls.fix: Return an effect or let dirty-window projection run after reduction.

common-pitfalls.pitfall: Expecting embedded or modal window policies to work in v1.
common-pitfalls.fix: Use singleton, per-key, or free-instance for now.
